stories.headline: California Signs Landmark Anthropic Deal, Bringing Claude to All State Agencies at Half Price
stories.key_takeaway: California became the first state to deploy a frontier AI model at scale, giving roughly 300,000 state workers and any opt-in city or county access to Anthropic's Claude at a 50 percent discount through a new centralized procurement portal.

pull_quote.text: As a California company, we feel a real responsibility to our home state. We're honored to expand our partnership with California's agencies and to put Claude to work for the people who keep this state running.

stories.article_excerpt: When Gov. Gavin Newsom stepped to the microphone in Sacramento on June 29, he did not announce a pilot or another task force.

stories.headline: National Grid Bets $1.75 Billion on AI Data Center Power With Joulent Stake
stories.key_takeaway: National Grid's $1.75 billion, 35% stake in Joulent marks utilities becoming direct financial owners of AI power generation, as electricity, not chips, emerges as the binding constraint on the AI buildout.

pull_quote.text: Our investment in Joulent is a disciplined, partner-led investment in contracted critical infrastructure for the AI-driven large load economy.

stories.article_excerpt: Britain's National Grid, one of the oldest and most conservative names in global utilities, is placing a $1.75 billion wager.

stories.headline: Bloom Energy and Brookfield Expand AI Power Partnership Fivefold to $25 Billion
stories.key_takeaway: Brookfield quintupled its AI-power financing framework with Bloom Energy from $5 billion to $25 billion, treating on-site fuel-cell generation as a way to deliver dependable megawatts to data centers faster than multi-year grid interconnection queues allow.

pull_quote.text: When we formed this partnership, we said it was the first phase of a much larger vision. Today's commitment reflects the momentum we are seeing in the market.

stories.article_excerpt: When Bloom Energy and Brookfield first agreed to build and finance fuel-cell power for AI data centers, the number was $5 billion.

stories.headline: Canadian Defense Startup Dominion Dynamics Raises $100 Million in Record Series A
stories.key_takeaway: Ottawa's Dominion Dynamics raised a record $139M CAD ($100M USD) Series A to build AI-driven Arctic defense systems, signaling the allied defense-tech wave that produced Anduril is now spreading to NATO markets pursuing sovereign capability.

pull_quote.text: Canada once built technology the rest of the world wanted, then convinced itself that was someone else's role. We started Dominion to show the capability never left, and this round lets us build at the scale and speed the moment demands.

stories.article_excerpt: Ottawa-based Dominion Dynamics has closed a $139-million CAD Series A, described as Canada's largest defense-tech round.

stories.headline: Verkada Partners With Nvidia to Push Physical AI Into Buildings and Security Systems
stories.key_takeaway: Verkada took an Nvidia investment and technical partnership to turn its 2.4 million security cameras and sensors into physical-AI endpoints, deepening Nvidia's move into the built environment while raising fresh surveillance and governance questions.

pull_quote.text: Verkada has been building and deploying Physical AI before the term existed. With our footprint of more than 2.4 million devices across 170 countries and 30,000 organizations, we've proven that the built environment is one of the largest beneficiaries of AI.

stories.article_excerpt: Verkada, the cloud-based physical security company whose cameras and sensors watch over 30,000 organizations, is going deeper on AI.

stories.headline: Google Releases Gemini 3.1 Flash Image and Gemini 3 Pro Image as It Fills the Lineup Awaiting 3.5 Pro
stories.key_takeaway: Google shipped Gemini 3.1 Flash Image and Gemini 3 Pro Image on June 30 to keep its release cadence going while its flagship Gemini 3.5 Pro keeps slipping over enterprise complaints about token consumption in agentic tasks.

pull_quote.text: The two image models are separate models in the Gemini lineup that do not substitute for the 3.5 Pro launch.

stories.article_excerpt: Google shipped two image models on Tuesday, and neither one was the model everybody is waiting for.

stories.headline: OpenAI to Run GPT-5.6 Sol on Cerebras at 750 Tokens a Second, Chasing Real-Time AI
stories.key_takeaway: OpenAI plans to serve its GPT-5.6 Sol flagship on Cerebras wafer-scale hardware at up to 750 tokens per second in July, roughly 15x faster than typical GPU serving, signaling that inference speed is becoming the frontier's new competitive axis.

pull_quote.text: We're also launching GPT-5.6 Sol on Cerebras at up to 750 tokens per second in July, bringing frontier intelligence to customers at unprecedented speed.

stories.article_excerpt: When OpenAI unveiled its GPT-5.6 model family on June 26, the headline benchmark scores were not the most consequential number.

stories.headline: xAI's Grok 4.3 Arrives on Amazon Bedrock as the Cheapest Major-Lab Model on AWS
stories.key_takeaway: xAI's Grok 4.3 debuts on Amazon Bedrock at $1.25/$2.50 per million tokens, making it the cheapest major-lab model on AWS and intensifying the enterprise AI price war just as budgets tighten.

pull_quote.text: Grok 4.3 sits on the Pareto frontier for intelligence versus cost, delivering 2-10x more intelligence per dollar than other frontier models.
pull_quote.role: Company announcement, Grok on Amazon Bedrock

stories.article_excerpt: When xAI slipped Grok 4.3 onto Amazon Bedrock in mid-June, it did not arrive as a headline-grabbing frontier launch.

stories.headline: Anthropic's Claude Goes Generally Available in Microsoft Foundry on Nvidia GB300 Blackwell Ultra
stories.key_takeaway: Claude is now generally available in Microsoft Foundry on Azure, running on Nvidia's newest GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPUs, giving enterprises a Microsoft-governed third path to Anthropic's models and deepening the Microsoft-Nvidia-Anthropic alliance even as Microsoft remains a major OpenAI backer.

pull_quote.text: With Claude now available in Microsoft Foundry running on NVIDIA GB300 GPUs, more organizations can run advanced, specialized AI agents with the performance, scale and security needed for production.
pull_quote.role: VP and GM of Enterprise Computing, Nvidia

stories.article_excerpt: Enterprise AI rarely stalls on model quality. It stalls on procurement, governance sign-offs and data residency.

stories.headline: 'Squidbleed': Claude Mythos Uncovers a 29-Year-Old Memory Leak in the Squid Proxy
stories.key_takeaway: An AI model in Anthropic's Project Glasswing found a 29-year-old credential-leaking bug in the Squid proxy that survived three decades of human review, showcasing both the promise and the double-edged risk of AI-driven vulnerability discovery.

pull_quote.text: Few developers would guess that searching for '\0' succeeds, which may explain how a one-line bug survived close to 30 years of code review.

stories.article_excerpt: For 29 years, a single line of C code in one of the internet's most widely deployed proxy servers quietly leaked secrets.

stories.headline: KAIST's VOTP Teaches Robots Human Judgment From a Handful of Preference Videos
stories.key_takeaway: KAIST's ICML 2026 Oral-selected VOTP method learns a robot's reward function from about ten preference videos instead of tens of thousands of human labels, sharply cutting the annotation cost that bottlenecks physical AI and RLHF.

pull_quote.text: Since VOTP can learn human judgment criteria with only a small number of videos, it is a core technology that will accelerate the era of robots making human-like judgments.
pull_quote.role: Professor, School of Electrical Engineering, KAIST

stories.article_excerpt: Teaching a robot what humans actually want has long been one of the most expensive problems in artificial intelligence.

stories.headline: Anthropic Launches Claude Science, a Dedicated AI App for Drug Discovery and Biology
stories.key_takeaway: Anthropic launched Claude Science, a dedicated AI workbench for drug discovery and biology, backed by a $400M Coefficient Bio acquisition and Nobel laureate John Jumper, escalating a three-way AI-for-science race with OpenAI and Google.

pull_quote.text: It's going to be a general purpose technology that helps us to make sense of that complexity, in its full complexity, better. We don't know for sure if that's going to work out. But I think we're seeing the beginnings of it.

stories.article_excerpt: When Dario Amodei stood up in San Francisco on Tuesday, he did not pitch Anthropic's newest product as a chatbot with a lab coat.

stories.headline: Five Eyes Issue Rare Joint Warning: AI-Powered Cyberattacks Are Months Away, Not Years
stories.key_takeaway: All five Five Eyes intelligence agencies jointly warned on June 22, 2026 that frontier AI models will transform offensive and defensive cyber capabilities within months, not years, and urged boards to treat cyber resilience as a leadership-level accountability.

pull_quote.text: Frontier AI models are anticipated to exceed current industry expectations, fundamentally transforming both offensive and defensive cyber capabilities. The timeline is not years, it is months.

stories.article_excerpt: Spy agencies do not usually agree on the wording of a press release, let alone on a deadline.

stories.headline: UN Scientific Panel Warns Unchecked AI Progress Could Create Catastrophic Risks

sources.name: UN Independent Scientific Panel on AI

stories.key_takeaway: A UN-backed independent scientific panel of 40 experts warned that AI now outpaces both scientific understanding and government oversight, with no guarantee that increasingly autonomous systems will not cause catastrophic harm.

pull_quote.text: AI capabilities are outpacing both scientific understanding and governments' ability to adapt. With growing evidence of deceptive AI behaviour, science currently cannot guarantee that as capabilities continue to increase, AI will not cause catastrophic harm.
pull_quote.role: Co-Chair, Independent International Scientific Panel on AI

data_points.label: US share of top AI compute

stories.article_excerpt: The world's first fully independent, UN-backed scientific body on artificial intelligence delivered its verdict on Wednesday.

stories.headline: EU and Apple Clash Over Siri AI Delay as the Digital Markets Act Meets Generative AI
stories.key_takeaway: Apple CEO Tim Cook and EU tech chief Henna Virkkunen held 'constructive' talks after Apple delayed its Gemini-powered Siri AI in Europe, making the fight the first real test of how the Digital Markets Act's interoperability rules apply to flagship generative-AI features.

pull_quote.text: The decision not to roll out Siri AI in the EU is Apple's and Apple's only because absolutely nothing in the DMA prohibits Apple from introducing new products in the EU.

data_points.label: Max DMA fine of global turnover

stories.article_excerpt: The most consequential meeting in the generative-AI industry this week was not a product launch. It was a video call.

stories.headline: Bank of England Official Says AI Trading Systems May Need 'Kill Switches'
stories.key_takeaway: Bank of England deputy governor Sarah Breeden warned that autonomous agentic AI trading systems could herd and amplify market volatility at machine speed, and said regulators may need market-wide kill switches because existing frameworks were never built for AI that acts on its own.

pull_quote.text: Our frameworks were not built to contemplate autonomous agents, and relying on a human in the loop for all agent actions is unlikely to be realistic.
pull_quote.role: Deputy Governor for Financial Stability, Bank of England

stories.article_excerpt: Speaking to a room of central bankers in Sintra, Portugal, one of the Bank of England's most senior officials floated a kill switch.
